formatted_address: 14132 Trenton Ave, Orland Park, IL 60462, USA
pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 12 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 17.68° → 4:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: Subject home is a single-story ranch with a full hip roof (no gable ends, rake = 0). The plan is L-shaped: a main rectangular body (~280 px E-W × 205 px N-S = ~51 × 38 ft) plus a NE-projecting section (~185 px × 140 px = ~34 × 26 ft), likely an attached garage. Eave polygon traced at 8 vertices using the orange bounding box as reference (box spans ~347 × 275 px = 63.5 × 50.3 ft). Shoelace formula on the pixel polygon yields ~73,100 sq px × 0.033504 = 2,449 sq ft (~77% of bounding box, consistent with L-shape hip fill). Eaves summed around the full exterior perimeter (~222 ft). Ridges estimated from visible centerline peaks: ~15 ft main body + ~10 ft NE section. Hip lines estimated from 45° plan geometry: 4 corners on main body (~26.5 ft each) + 2 exposed corners on NE section (~18 ft each) = ~106 + 36 ft, rounded to 105 ft total. Valley where NE section meets main body estimated at ~26 ft. White vehicle visible at north end is parked in driveway and excluded. Confidence is moderate due to tree shadow and oblique view partially obscuring some eave corners.

findings.description: A bright white rectangular object, approximately 4–6 ft in its longest dimension, is visible on the north-facing slope near the peak. It is clearly distinct in color and texture from the surrounding tan/beige shingles and does not match any other rooftop element on this roof.
findings.location_description: North slope, near the ridge, center of the roof.

condition.rationale: The subject home has a hip-style roof covered in tan/beige shingles. Overall shingle color is uniform across all visible slopes, all ridge lines are geometrically straight with no detectable sag, and no moss, debris, tarps, or obvious missing shingle sections are visible. The one notable finding is a bright white rectangular object on the north slope near the ridge. It is clearly differentiated from the surrounding shingle field and does not match the tone or texture of the rest of the roof. At this resolution it cannot be positively identified as a skylight, HVAC curb unit, or a patching/covering material — it warrants a closer look during in-person inspection. The scattered small dark dots across the field are consistent with standard pipe penetrations/vents and are not flagged as concerns.

fence_reason: vision roof area 2582 differs from solar 3170 by 18.5% (>12%)
